tags.name: Text Generation Inference
tags.description: Hugging Face Text Generation Inference API

post.summary: Generate tokens if `stream == false` or a stream of token if `stream == true`
Generate a stream of token using Server-Sent Events

include_usage.description: If set, an additional chunk will be streamed before the data: [DONE] message. The usage field on this chunk shows the token usage statistics for the entire request, and the choices field will always be an empty array. All other chunks will also include a usage field, but with a null value

inputs.example: My name is Olivier and I

name.description: Optional name identifier for the schema

ToolChoiceToolChoiceOneOf12.description: Means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message

content.example: My name is David and I

top_p.description:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the
tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered

frequency_penalty.description:
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far,
decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim

stop.description: Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the chat completion

temperature.description:
What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while
lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or `top_p` but not both

model.description:
UNUSED
ID of the model to use. See the model endpoint compatibility table for details on which models work with the Chat API

suffix.description:
The text to append to the prompt. This is useful for completing sentences or generating a paragraph of text.
please see the completion_template field in the model's tokenizer_config.json file for completion template

top_logprobs.description:
An integer between 0 and 5 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with
an associated log probability. logprobs must be set to true if this parameter is used

logit_bias.description:
UNUSED
Modify the likelihood of specified tokens appearing in the completion. Accepts a JSON object that maps tokens
(specified by their token ID in the tokenizer) to an associated bias value from -100 to 100. Mathematically,
the bias is added to the logits generated by the model prior to sampling. The exact effect will vary per model,
but values between -1 and 1 should decrease or increase likelihood of selection; values like -100 or 100 should
result in a ban or exclusive selection of the relevant token

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the chat completion

presence_penalty.description:
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far,
increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics

tool_prompt.description: A prompt to be appended before the tools
tool_prompt.example: Given the functions available, please respond with a JSON for a function call with its proper arguments that best answers the given prompt. Respond in the format {name: function name, parameters: dictionary of argument name and its value}.Do not use variables.

tools.description:
A list of tools the model may call. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. Use this to provide a list of
functions the model may generate JSON inputs for

n.description:
UNUSED
How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message. Note that you will be charged based on the
number of generated tokens across all of the choices. Keep n as 1 to minimize costs

logprobs.description:
Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true, returns the log probabilities of each
output token returned in the content of message

top_p.description:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the
tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered

frequency_penalty.description:
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far,
decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim

stop.description: Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens

temperature.description:
What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while
lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.

We generally recommend altering this or `top_p` but not both

messages.description: A list of messages comprising the conversation so far

model.description: [UNUSED] ID of the model to use. See the model endpoint compatibility table for details on which models work with the Chat API

watermark.description: Watermarking with [A Watermark for Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.10226)

frequency_penalty.description:
The parameter for frequency penalty. 1.0 means no penalty
Penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far,
decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim

best_of.description: Generate best_of sequences and return the one if the highest token logprobs

stop.description: Stop generating tokens if a member of `stop` is generated

truncate.description: Truncate inputs tokens to the given size

top_n_tokens.description: The number of highest probability vocabulary tokens to keep for top-n-filtering

max_new_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

decoder_input_details.description: Whether to return decoder input token logprobs and ids

top_k.description: The number of highest probability vocabulary tokens to keep for top-k-filtering

temperature.description: The value used to module the logits distribution

typical_p.description:
Typical Decoding mass
See [Typical Decoding for Natural Language Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2202.00666) for more information

return_full_text.description: Whether to prepend the prompt to the generated text

repetition_penalty.description:
The parameter for repetition penalty. 1.0 means no penalty.
See [this paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1909.05858.pdf) for more details

value.description:
A string that represents a [JSON Schema](https://json-schema.org/).

JSON Schema is a declarative language that allows to annotate JSON documents
with types and descriptions

inputs.example: My name is Olivier and I

ToolChoiceToolChoiceToolChoiceOneOf123.description: Means the model must call one or more tools

ToolChoiceOneOf1.description: Means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools
